label: baseline
notes: greedy toxicity low across all slices, but sampled (K=16) reveals much higher latent toxicity especially on rtp_challenging (support_rate=0.84, mean_max=0.775) -- plausible detox target, real gap between greedy and sampled behavior

label: sft
notes: vanilla SFT round A (baseline documentation): greedy toxicity collapsed near-zero across all slices but so did diversity -- 40% unique (30/75), single completion 'I'm sorry, I don't understand.' covers 45% (34/75) of all outputs. Tracked 35-prompt eyeball: 34/35 collapsed to a hedge template regardless of prompt content/toxicity bucket, only 1 genuine answer. Classic warning-sign pattern (toxicity near-zero AND uniqueness collapsing together) -- this is the documented vanilla-data baseline failure mode, not the foundation DPO/RM/PPO will build on.

label: sft_diverse
notes: Round B on sft_diverse.jsonl: exact-string uniqueness solved (75/75, 35/35 vs Round A's 40%/1 dominant string) but semantic collapse persists -- 32/35 tracked prompts still open with a refusal template regardless of content, incl. benign ones, vs 34/35 in Round A
